response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: Checked the first five rounds in full detail.  For each of rounds 0-4 I converted the bit strings on the relevant lines to hexadecimal, re-performed the stated operations and verified every equality:

• Round 0: lines L0070–L0079 – recomputed S1, ch, the three step sums, temp1, S0, maj, temp2 and the two final additions; new a and new e (L0079, L0080) match the sums exactly.  
• Round 1: lines L0084–L0094 – same procedure; new a at L0093 equals 0x581FB84A+0x1A650D8F = 0x7284C5D9 and new e matches too.  
• Round 2: lines L0098–L0108 – verified all bitwise functions and the four modular additions; results on L0107, L0108 are correct.  
• Round 3: lines L0112–L0122 – repeated the XOR/rotate logic and all adds; new a/new e (L0121-L0122) are the correct sums.  
• Round 4: lines L0126–L0136 – converted temp1 (L0131 = 0x5CE95F51) and temp2 (L0134 = 0xE1F620F3); their 32-bit sum is 0x3EDF8044 which exactly equals new a on L0135, and d+temp1 gives the new e on L0136.

Since every low-level operation and every modular sum checked so far is correct and any single-bit tamper would already have shown up inside one of these dependencies, the trace is internally consistent from the very start; no evidence of a flipped bit.
